result.diagram_code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant I as Attacker
    participant B as Responder (xB = B)
    participant S as Server

    Note over I,B: Attacker knows B and pkB from public outputs
    I->>B: B
    I->>B: encrypt((NY, B), pkB)
    activate B
    Note over B: beginAparam(B,B)
    B->>S: (B, B)
    S-->>B: sign((pkB, B), skS)
    Note over B: beginAfull(B,B,pkB,pkB,NY,Nb)
    B-->>I: encrypt((NY, Nb, B), pkB)
    I->>B: encrypt(NY, pkB)
    Note over B: endBparam(B,B) and endBfull(...)
    B-->>I: sencrypt(secretBNa, NY)
    B-->>I: sencrypt(secretBNb, Nb)
    deactivate B
    I->>I: sdecrypt(sencrypt(secretBNa, NY), NY)
    Note over I: Learns secretBNa
result.explanation: The responder can be completed by the attacker without any matching initiator run. In the trace, the attacker starts B as responder, sends message 3 as encrypt((NY,B), pkB), gets back message 6, and then finishes with encrypt(NY, pkB). This works because the responder's final check compares the returned value with NY (the nonce supplied by the attacker in message 3) instead of Nb (the fresh nonce generated by the responder). Since the attacker already knows NY, it can satisfy the check, trigger endBparam/endBfull, and obtain sencrypt(secretBNa, NY), which it can decrypt because it also knows NY. That is why secretBNa secrecy fails and the B-side correspondence queries fail.
result.patch_suggestion:
In processResponder, change the message-7 check to validate the responder's fresh nonce Nb, not the received nonce NY. A minimal local fix is:

replace
  if nonce_to_bitstring(NY) = decrypt(m3, skB) then
with
  if nonce_to_bitstring(Nb) = decrypt(m3, skxB) then

This makes message 7 match the protocol intent ({Nb}_pkB), so an attacker who only chose NY can no longer complete the responder alone.
result.notes: The provided attack is fully explained by the wrong nonce check in the responder. There are also hardcoded key uses elsewhere (for example skA/skB instead of role-selected keys), but they are not needed to explain the shown counterexample.
